source_jobs.title: Mechanical Engineering Fall Co-Op
source_jobs.description:
HaloBraid is a hair technology company using breakthrough engineering to elevate everyday routines. We’re building the world’s first robotic hair-braiding system—integrating computer vision, robotics, compliant fiber manipulation, and novel mechatronic machines. This is one of the hardest unsolved problems in robotics, applied to a $300B industry.



8 billion hours are spent braiding hair each year. HaloBraid gives that time back. The system collaborates with professional stylists to reduce braiding appointments from 6 hours to minutes and grow their business. We’re moving from R&D into deployment with salons, well-funded by leading investors. This is a unique opportunity to shape the future of the hair styling industry.

We are looking for a Fall 2026 Mechanical Engineering Co-op to play a pivotal role in developing our innovative hair braiding robotic system.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - ﻿﻿Contribute to the development of complex technology, product sub-assemblies, and test rigs

 - ﻿﻿Take an active, hands-on role to ensure that engineering problems are resolved thoroughly

 - ﻿﻿Utilize various rapid prototyping technologies to build, test, and iterate

 - ﻿﻿Use hand calculations, computer simulation, and/or lab-based testing to validate designs through the development process

 - ﻿﻿Conduct user testing and project the voice of the customer back into the design

 - Conduct various forms of testing, problem solving, and design iteration


REQUIREMENTS:

 - ﻿﻿At least one semester remaining in your undergraduate or graduate degree

 - ﻿﻿Experience with CAD (we use Onshape, experience with Solidworks or equivalent is acceptable)

 - ﻿﻿Experience with various rapid prototyping methods (3D printing, CNC, etc)

 - ﻿﻿Demonstrated design intelligence, mechanical intuition, creative problem solving, and strong attention to detail

 - ﻿﻿A willingness to fail and learn!
   
   


DESIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE (OPTIONAL BUT THE MORE, THE BETTER)

 - ﻿﻿Gone through (and documented!) the design process for a project, through school or on your own time

 - ﻿﻿Strong documentation skills and the ability to convey complex ideas in a team setting

 - Strong experimental rigor and testing process

 - Experience with complex CAD models with clear top down design

 - ﻿﻿Experience with consumer electronics, robotics, or hair styling tools

 - ﻿﻿Interest in hair braiding/styling

 - Interest in robotics and mechanisms

 - Complementary engineering experience:
   
   - Mechanisms, mechatronics, and moving components.
   
   - ﻿﻿Experience with design optimization using FEA/CFD simulation
   
   - Electronics prototyping and soldering
     
     

Working Style

 - User centric, you want to build stuff people use and love

 - Low ego, adaptable, and collaborative spirit

 - Willing to learn through fast-paced iteration and feedback

 - Passionate, driven by innovation
   
   

Benefits of Working with Us:

 - Company subsidized parking or commute for onsite employees



Onsite work

This is an onsite full time position at our office/lab in Cambridge, MA. You must have authorization to work in the US.



Don’t Meet Every Requirement?

If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align with every single part of the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway.



Equal Opportunity Employer:

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, sex, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all employment practices within our organization.

source_jobs.title: Senior Robotics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Halo is a technology company building the future of haircare for all textures through exceptional engineering and human-centered design. We create category-defining products built for the complexities others overlook. We're building HaloBraid, the world’s first braid assist device. It helps stylists finish braids 5 times faster, flawlessly and gently. This is one of the hardest unsolved problems in robotics, applied to the $270B salon services industry, and integrating computer vision, compliant fiber manipulation, and novel mechanisms.



Billions of hours are spent braiding hair each year. HaloBraid gives that time back. We’re moving from R&D into deployment with salons, and backed by leading investors Seven Seven Six, AlleyCorp, and Bling Capital.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Refine embedded systems and vision algorithms for a unique robotic/automation product, with a focus on robust solutions and efficient real-time algorithms

 - Implement, test, debug, and document embedded systems design

 - Collaborate with mechanical, design, and electrical engineers, as well as users





REQUIREMENTS

 - Experience with motion control for a variety of actuator types (e.g. stepper motors, servo motors, brushed and brushless DC motors)

 - Experience with Python and Embedded C/C++

 - Experience implementing feedback control systems

 - Experience with complex embedded systems

 - Experience with computer vision, including system architecture, software development, integration, and testing

 - Exposure to industry standard communication protocols (e.g. I2C, SPI, UART)


DESIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE (OPTIONAL BUT THE MORE, THE BETTER)

 - Experience in implementing robust perception and computer vision solutions in high speed low-latency applications.

 - Experience with wireless communication protocols (e.g. Wi-Fi, 4G, Bluetooth, MQTT)

 - Complementary engineering experience:
   
   - Electronics prototyping and design
   
   - Machine learning
   
   - Software development
   
   - Mechanical design



Working Style

 - User centric, you want to build stuff people use and love

 - Low ego, adaptable, and collaborative spirit

 - Willing to learn through fast-paced iteration and feedback

 - Passionate, driven by innovation
   
   

Benefits of Working with Us:

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision benefits; 100% employee medical premiums covered

 - Meaningful equity

 - 12 company holidays and unlimited PTO

 - Company subsidized parking or commute for onsite employees



Onsite work

This is an onsite position at our office/lab in Boston, MA. You must have authorization to work in the US.



Don’t Meet Every Requirement?

If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align with every single part of the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway.

Equal Opportunity Employer:

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, sex, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all employment practices within our organization.

source_jobs.title: Senior Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Halo is a technology company building the future of haircare for all textures through exceptional engineering and human-centered design. We create category-defining products built for the complexities others overlook. We're building HaloBraid, the world’s first braid assist device. It helps stylists finish braids 5 times faster, flawlessly and gently. This is one of the hardest unsolved problems in robotics, applied to a $270B salon services industry, and integrating computer vision, compliant fiber manipulation, and novel mechanisms.



Billions of hours are spent braiding hair each year. HaloBraid gives that time back. We’re moving from R&D into deployment with salons, and backed by leading investors Seven Seven Six, AlleyCorp, and Bling Capital.



Responsibilities:

 - Architect the design of mechanical systems and sub-assemblies from 3D CAD to production-ready parts.

 - Collaborate with mechanical, industrial design, electrical, and manufacturing engineers, users, and cross-functional teams.

 - Manage engineers, set best practices and standards, and strengthen our engineering culture

 - Push for elegant and effective solutions, challenge status quo, and drive design forward.

 - This is a hands-on engineering role that reports directly to the CEO.
   
   

Requirements

 - Experience with CAD and mechanical design of complex robotics or consumer electronics, and direct ownership of systems or subsystems

 - Experience with all stages of the concept to production cycle (rapid prototyping, DFM, contract manufacturing)

 - Experience bringing novel technology to market
   
   

Desired Skills & Experience (optional but the more, the better)

 - Experience with mechanisms, mechatronics, and moving components.

 - Complementary engineering experience:
   
   - DFM/DFA principles and mass manufacturing methods
   
   - Design optimization integrating CFD simulation results
   
   - Electronics prototyping

 - Experience collaborating with suppliers at the prototype and contract manufacturing stage.

 - Experience managing CAD systems and general assemblies

 - Experience working with industrial design to incorporate user needs, ergonomics, and aesthetics



Working Style

 - User centric, you want to build stuff people use and love

 - Low ego, adaptable, and collaborative spirit

 - Excited to learn through fast-paced iteration and user feedback

 - Passionate, driven by innovation

 - Excellent communication, an ability to concisely convey and discuss complex ideas



Benefits of Working with Us:

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision benefits

 - 100% medical premium coverage

 - Meaningful equity

 - 12 company holidays and unlimited PTO

 - Company subsidized parking or commute for onsite employees
   
   

Onsite Work

This is an onsite position at our office/lab in Cambridge, MA. You must have authorization to work in the US.

Don’t Meet Every Requirement?

If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align with every single part of the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway.

Equal Opportunity Employer:

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, sex, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all employment practices within our organization.

source_jobs.title: Social Media Associate
source_jobs.description:
About Us

HaloBraid is a technology company using breakthrough engineering to help people perform routines faster. We’re building the world’s first robotic hair-braiding that works with professional stylists to reduce braiding appointments from hours to minutes and grow their business. Billion hours are spent braiding hair each year. HaloBraid exists to give that time back.

Our product has already gone viral, generating millions of views across social platforms, building a fast-growing community of stylists, braid wearers, beauty lovers, and early believers excited for what’s next. This role will help transform our early traction into a dedicated and nurtured community.

We’re looking for a social media-obsessed highly creative Social Media Associate to help bring HaloBraid to life across social platforms and in the community. This is a hands-on role for someone who loves creating content, following trends, shooting and editing short-form video, writing captions, engaging with audiences, and building digital community from the ground up.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own HaloBraid’s day-to-day organic social strategy, including content calendars, platform planning, posting cadence, and creative testing.

 - Concept, create, shoot, edit, caption, and publish social content across TikTok, Instagram, Reels, YouTube Shorts, and emerging platforms.

 - Identify trends, sounds, memes, conversations, and cultural moments that HaloBraid can participate in authentically.

 - Develop recurring content ideas and series that build awareness, trust, community, and excitement ahead of launch.

 - Capture behind-the-scenes content with the HaloBraid team, stylists, community members, product demos, events, and brand moments.

 - Own day-to-day community engagement, including comments, DMs, tagged posts, mentions, and social listening.

 - Track social performance, report on KPIs, and use data to adjust strategy, improve content, and drive growth.

 - Support community events, interviews, creator moments, and brand storytelling across the broader marketing and community team.
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1–3 years of experience in social media, content creation, community, influencer marketing, brand marketing, or a related role.

 - Loves social media, is chronically online and enjoys keeping up with trends.

 - Deep fluency in TikTok, Instagram, Reels, YouTube Shorts, and emerging social platforms.

 - Strong short-form video instincts. You understand hooks, pacing, captions, trends, sounds, memes, and what makes people share.

 - Hands-on content creation experience, including concepting, shooting, editing, writing copy, and posting.

 - Comfortable being on camera and helping others feel comfortable on camera too.

 - Passion for beauty, hair care, hair trends, textured hair, braiding, salons, and community.

 - Strong taste level and an understanding of what feels fresh, premium, funny, culturally relevant, and socially native.

 - Independent, organized, proactive, and excited to work in a fast-paced startup environment.




NICE TO HAVES

 - Internship or work experience at a beauty, hair care, fashion, lifestyle, consumer, or creator-led brand.

 - Experience creating or growing your own TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, or other platform.

 - Experience with CapCut, Canva, Figma, Adobe Premiere, Photoshop, Lightroom, or similar tools.
   
   

Working Style

 - Low ego, adaptable, and collaborative spirit

 - Excited to learn in a fast-paced environment

 - Passionate and community drive
   
   

Benefits of Working with Us:

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision benefits

 - 100% medical premium coverage

 - Meaningful equity

 - 12 company holidays and unlimited PTO

 - Company subsidized parking or commute for onsite employees
   
   

Remote / Hybrid Work

HaloBraid is based in Cambridge, MA. We have a preference for candidates who are able to work in person with the team in the Boston area or NYC-based candidates who can work remot

notes: Highly technical roles, likely a startup with a strong engineering focus
